architectural_purpose: deterministic transition review session evidence packaging without transition execution

classification_semantics.blocked: governance, integrity, or execution-boundary preservation blocks the session
classification_semantics.complete: session identity, evaluation records, provenance, replay, rollback, explainability, and evidence are present with no non-safe markers
classification_semantics.incomplete: session identity, evaluation records, provenance, continuity, or explainability evidence is missing
classification_semantics.partially_complete: at least one evaluation is complete while one or more required session guarantees remain visible
classification_semantics.prohibited: requested session recording behavior violates non-execution boundaries
classification_semantics.unknown: session semantics, provenance meaning, continuity meaning, or evidence meaning is uncertain
classification_semantics.unsupported: session domain or evidence is outside supported deterministic scope

explicit_limitations: v3.9 Phase 5 transition session intelligence is non-executable
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable orchestration execution
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable transition execution
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable graph traversal
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable routing, scheduling, or dispatch
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable runtime orchestration engines
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable runtime mutation
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable authorization or approval systems
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable optimization or recommendations
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable ranking, scoring, or selection
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable autonomous behavior
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable callable orchestration flows
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable transition handlers or runtime state machines
v3.9 Phase 5 does not enable production behavior

finding_semantics.continuity: session replay or rollback continuity finding
